message.content:
**Pre‑Launch Website Checklist (Marketing Site – WordPress or Similar)**  

---

### 1. Hosting, Domain & SSL
- Domain points to correct server (A/AAAA, CNAME, www/non‑www canonical choice)  
- SSL certificate installed and valid (no mixed-content warnings)  
- Force HTTPS and preferred domain (www vs non‑www) via redirects  
- Time zone, locale, language set correctly in CMS and server  
- Email sending configured (transactional / contact forms): SMTP or API (SPF, DKIM, DMARC records)  

---

### 2. Platform & Core Configuration (WordPress or Similar)
- CMS, theme, and all plugins updated to latest stable versions  
- Remove unused themes/plugins; no abandoned or insecure plugins  
- Set site title, tagline, favicon, and logo correctly  
- Permalink structure SEO‑friendly (e.g., `/post-name/`)  
- Disable “discourage search engines” / dev mode / maintenance flags  
- Global typography, colors, buttons consistent in theme/customizer  
- Multilingual setup (if applicable): languages, switcher, correct default  

---

### 3. Design & UX
- Responsive behavior checked on mobile, tablet, desktop (key breakpoints)  
- Cross‑browser test: Chrome, Safari, Firefox, Edge (latest versions)  
- Navigation clear and intuitive; header and footer consistent on all pages  
- Breadcrumbs enabled (if appropriate)  
- Clear primary CTAs above the fold on key pages  
- Forms easy to find and simple to complete; error messages clear  
- Contrast ratios accessible; text sizes legible; focus states visible  
- No obvious layout shifts or overlapping elements  

---

### 4. Content & Copy
- All core pages ready: Home, About, Services/Products, Pricing, Blog, Contact, Legal pages  
- Copy proofread for spelling, grammar, style, and brand voice  
- Value proposition and key benefits clearly articulated on Home and key landing pages  
- Headlines and subheadings scannable and meaningful  
- Contact info correct (phone, email, address, social links) and consistent across site  
- Testimonials, case studies, portfolio items formatted and credible (names, roles, companies, permissions)  
- Blog structure set (categories, tags, archive pages) with at least a starter set of posts  
- Avoid placeholder/lorem ipsum, dummy images, and default template text  

---

### 5. Images, Media & Assets
- Images optimized and compressed (WebP/AVIF/JPEG as appropriate)  
- Dimensions set correctly (no huge originals scaled down via CSS only)  
- Alt text added for all decorative and functional images (descriptive, not keyword stuffing)  
- Hero and background images tested for load time and clarity on mobile  
- Video hosting chosen (YouTube, Vimeo, or self‑hosted) and embeds responsive  
- Icons and fonts loaded efficiently (subset custom fonts if needed)  

---

### 6. Technical Performance
- Page speed tested (Lighthouse/PageSpeed Insights, GTmetrix, WebPageTest)  
- Caching plugin or server‑side caching configured (page, object, browser caching)  
- GZIP or Brotli compression enabled  
- CSS/JS minification and concatenation (where safe); remove unused assets  
- Use a CDN for static assets if needed (images, JS, CSS)  
- Limit heavy third‑party scripts (chats, widgets, tag managers, fonts)  
- Core Web Vitals checked: LCP, CLS, INP/FID within recommended thresholds  

---

### 7. Site Structure & Internal Linking
- Clear hierarchy: Home → Sections → Services/Products → Detail pages  
- Clean, human‑readable URLs with keywords where natural  
- Logical internal linking between related pages and posts  
- No orphan pages (every page reachable from at least one other)  
- Pagination working correctly on blog/archive pages  
- 404 page customized with helpful navigation and search  

---

### 8. SEO Fundamentals
- Unique, descriptive title tag and meta description for every key page  
- H1 used once per page; heading hierarchy (H2/H3) logical and consistent  
- Image filenames and alt text aligned with content topic (not stuffed)  
- Canonical tags set correctly (handle duplicates, parameters, variations)  
- Robots.txt configured (block only what should be hidden)  
- XML sitemap generated and accessible (e.g., `/sitemap.xml`)  
- Noindex/nofollow tags used correctly (dev, thin, or private pages if needed)  
- Open Graph (og:) and Twitter Card meta tags set (global defaults + page‑specific for key pages)  
- Local SEO: NAP (Name, Address, Phone) consistent with Google Business Profile; local schema for business if
